candidates.role: first supervised fixed-ontology arm with an overlap-capable W2NER head
candidates.evidence: A directly relevant study on retyped Shen Bao articles from 1872-1947 reports F1 58.26 for MacBERT versus 56.83 for SIKU. It is target-period evidence but not an OCR-robustness result.

candidates.role: license-gated historical-encoder supervised arm with an identical W2NER head
candidates.evidence: An EvaHan controlled comparison reports W2NER average F1 88.48 versus GlobalPointer 87.33 and CRF 86.33; GujiRoBERTa-W2NER reached 86.34 on the public test. This is clean ancient text, and the checkpoint card declares no license.

candidates.role: historical-encoder supervised arm with an identical W2NER head
candidates.evidence: Pretrained on Siku Quanshu; the retyped Shen Bao comparison reports F1 56.83, below MacBERT's 58.26. Historical pretraining remains relevant, but neither result measures OCR transfer.
The 2026 preprint reports coverage of 100+ languages and average gains over GLiNER-X-base, but no historical, Traditional-Chinese, or OCR result; the checkpoint card has no license metadata.

Official model card reports zh_pud F1 0.6794; no historical, Traditional-Chinese, or OCR benchmark. A local first-50-region compatibility run required a fixed zh-hant Stanza splitter and produced 67 noisy candidates versus 5 from multi-v2.1, with only 2 exact span/type agreements; this does not establish accuracy.
Multimodal schema extraction with verbatim strings; no disclosed historical-Chinese NER result.
Chinese-capable 27B vision-language ceiling with stronger generic/document results than the earlier family; no historical NER evidence and roughly 55.6 GB BF16 weights.
Produced noisy candidate spans on one lossy Shen Bao page and has not passed review.

excluded_from_primary_benchmark.reason: Published language list does not include Chinese.
May remain a control, but no published Chinese evaluation supports prioritizing it over GLiNER-X.

Retain only as a lower-cost control when 80 GB-class hardware is unavailable; NuExtract3's own generic structured-extraction comparison favors NuExtract3, and neither has target historical-Chinese NER evidence.
